findings.title: Config: inactivity timeout can silently exceed the hard run ceiling — dead config
findings.suggested_fix: Add cross-validation after line 528: `if (runInactivityTimeoutMs > runTimeoutMs) { console.warn(...) }`.
evidence: GATEWAY_RUN_INACTIVITY_TIMEOUT_MS and GATEWAY_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS are validated independently as positive integers (config.ts:504-511, 513-528) with no cross-validation.

When inactivity > run timeout, the hard ceiling (AbortSignal.timeout) always fires first (run-core.ts:304-306). The inactivity timer at config.ts:513-528 with exceeded timeout is armed (run-core.ts:293-297) but never reaches its delay.

The hard ceiling signal is an independent source in AbortSignal.any (run-core.ts:304-307) -- never cleared, reset, or modified by any inactivity logic. run.ts:489 creates AbortSignal.timeout(remainingBudgetMs) once and never touches it again.
The inactivity-timeout kind exists in the error repertoire (run-core.ts:42, run.ts:673, run.ts:750-754) but never appears in logs when config is misaligned.

findings.title: AbortSignal.any composite signal retains listeners after normal completion — bounded memory retention
findings.suggested_fix: In the session.idle handler (line 607-608), add `inactivityController?.abort()` after `clearInactivity()` to trigger AbortSignal.any's spec-mandated cleanup of source listeners.
evidence: AbortSignal.any([signal, inactivityController.signal]) at run-core.ts:304-306 registers internal abort listeners on both source signals. Per spec, these listeners are only removed when the composite is aborted (one source fires).
When runCore completes via session.idle (line 601-608), clearInactivity() clears the timer but never aborts inactivityController. Neither source signal fires during normal completion.

The caller's AbortSignal.timeout(remainingBudgetMs) (run.ts:489) holds a strong reference to the composite's listener via addEventListener closure, keeping the composite and inactivityController alive.

Retention duration: until AbortSignal.timeout fires -- up to remainingBudgetMs (max 30 min after run completed). Objects retained: AbortController, composite AbortSignal, closure chains. Verified trace: run.ts:489 -> run-core.ts:304-307 -> run-core.ts:281-301.
No functional correctness issue -- no code path reads these objects after return. Minor GC pressure under high run concurrency.

findings.title: void iterator.return?.() leaves theoretical unhandledRejection path — latent process crash
findings.suggested_fix: Change `void iterator.return?.()` to `void iterator.return?.()?.catch(() => {})` at line 701. One extra token eliminates the entire crash class.
evidence: Change at run-core.ts:701: from `await iterator.return?.()` to `void iterator.return?.()`, with comment 'Do NOT await -- the inner iterator may be a never-resolving generator.' (lines 695-699). No .catch() on the discarded promise.
Current SSE iterator (serverSentEvents.gen.ts) has synchronous finally cleanup -- won't reject. But stream param is typed AsyncIterable<unknown> (line 653) -- any iterable qualifies.

If iterator.return?.() returns a rejected promise: no .catch() handler -> Node emits unhandledRejection. Gateway has zero process.on('unhandledRejection') handlers (confirmed by source search). Node 24 default --unhandled-rejection=throw terminates the process.
Fix is zero-cost defense-in-depth: the .catch(() => {}) handles both the hang case (still doesn't await) and the rejection case (swallows).

residual_risks.risk: Gateway has no unhandledRejection handler — any unhandled rejection from any code path crashes the process in Node 24 (default --unhandled-rejection=throw). Not scoped to this change, but the void-change increases the risk surface.
residual_risks.mitigation: Add `process.on('unhandledRejection', (reason) => { logger.error({err: String(reason)}, 'Unhandled rejection') })` at gateway entry point.

testing_gaps.gap: No test for makeAbortableStream's finally block with a rejecting iterator.return(). The void-change contract is uncovered.
testing_gaps.suggested_test: Create a mock AsyncIterable whose iterator.return() returns a rejected promise. Verify makeAbortableStream exits without throwing or crashing.

testing_gaps.gap: No test for simultaneous inactivity + hard ceiling abort. The prioritization logic at run-core.ts:623-631 (inactivity-timeout kind checked before timeout kind) has no coverage.
testing_gaps.suggested_test: Provide both a pre-aborted signal and a pre-armed inactivity controller. Verify error kind is 'inactivity-timeout'.

testing_gaps.gap: No config test for inactivityTimeoutMs > runTimeoutMs cross-validation.
testing_gaps.suggested_test: Set GATEWAY_RUN_INACTIVITY_TIMEOUT_MS > GATEWAY_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS. Verify warning or behavior.
